mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises by > 0.5% annually, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth above 100k in a month signals robust labor demand, increasing corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns remain negative in > 50% of triggered instances, or PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with SPX returns (Pearson r < 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and rising inflation signal stagflationary pressures, causing equity market sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls > 1.5% YoY and CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% MoM
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in > 40% of instances with INDPRO decline and CPI rise, or interaction effect size < 0.05 in regression models.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts below 1.3 million units signals weak demand, prompting negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in > 50% of triggered instances, or HOUST decline fails to predict SPX returns (p > 0.10 in OLS).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent CPI inflation above 3% triggers Fed rate hike expectations, reducing equity risk appetite and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in > 55% of instances with CPI > 3%, or CPI fails to explain SPX returns (R² < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real sales and housing starts indicates strong consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in > 45% of instances with RSAFS and HOUST growth, or interaction term coefficient < 0.05 in regression.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in unemployment below 3.5% signals labor market tightness, increasing wage pressures and causing negative S&P 500 returns during late-cycle regimes.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in > 50% of triggered instances, or UNRATE decline fails to correlate with SPX returns (Pearson r > -0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production signals strong supply-side growth, boosting corporate profits and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in > 45% of instances with INDPRO growth, or INDPRO fails to explain SPX returns (R² < 0.03).
